title: Oligarchy’s Ancient Origins
summary: The struggle between democracy and oligarchy in classical Greece was bitter, with oligarchies relying on skillfully designed political institutions to shore up unpopular regimes. As concerns grow over the “rule of the few” in the United States, Americans should consider how their own institutions are doing oligarchs’ w…

title: How Modi Has Remade India
summary: Over the last 12 years, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has overseen dizzying progress in economic modernization and state capacity-building, as well as dismaying setbacks on institutional independence and minority inclusion. This record offers important insights into how India will navigate the 21st century.

title: Water Makes the World Go Round
summary: Despite being vital to health, security, and economic prosperity, water is often taken for granted. As supplies come under growing strain, water-related considerations will shape economic policy and political decision-making, as well as firms’ investment decisions, risk assessments, and corporate strategies.

title: The Real Competitiveness Trap
summary: Policymakers obsessed with competitiveness both in Europe and beyond need to abandon old orthodoxies. New research finds that for two decades, capital in Europe has been abundant and cheap, and corporate profits strong, yet investment, productivity, and wages have stalled, and labor's share of total income has shrunk.
